metadata.summary: A Python package that fetches YouTube video view counts by video titles and optional artists using async http requests.

documentation:
# yt_viewfinder
 Python package designed to effortlessly fetch YouTube video view counts based on video titles and artists

## Features
- Fetches view counts for YouTube videos based on titles and optional artists.

## Installation
Install the package directly from PyPI:

```bash
pip install yt-viewfinder
```

## To Use

```
from yt_viewfinder import get_youtube_view_count

# Example: Get view count for "Thriller" by Michael Jackson
view_count = get_youtube_view_count("Thriller", "Michael Jackson")
print(f"View Count: {view_count}")
```
Output: 
if no matching video it returns 0

functions.name: yt_viewfinder.core.convert_view_count
documentation: Convert Youtube View Count to an integer

functions.name: yt_viewfinder.core._fetch_view_count
documentation: Fetch the view count of a Youtube video

functions.name: yt_viewfinder.core.get_youtube_view_count
documentation:
Get the view count of the first YouTube video matching the given title and optional artist.

Parameters:
    title (str): The title of the song/video.
    artist (str, optional): The artist of the song. Defaults to "".

Returns:
    int: The integer view count of the first matching YouTube video. Returns 0 if not found.

variables.name: yt_viewfinder.core.RETY_LIMIT
documentation: Number of times to retry a request
